leagueBriefing: Russell Westbrook made history with his 208th career triple-double in a Kings win, while the Celtics swept their season series against the Cavaliers as Jayson Tatum continues his comeback from an Achilles injury.

games.headline: Murphy and Bey power Pelicans as Wizards drop eighth straight
games.brief: The New Orleans Pelicans dominated the paint to pull away from the Washington Wizards, extending the capital city's losing skid to eight games. Despite a competitive first half where Washington rained in three-pointers, New Orleans exerted their will inside behind a balanced scoring attack and a poised performance from rookie Jeremiah Fears off the bench.
bullets: Trey Murphy III led the Pelicans with 24 points, while Saddiq Bey added 23 against his former team.
New Orleans dominated the interior, outscoring Washington 72-44 in the paint.
Wizards rookie Tre Johnson reached the 20-point mark for the fourth time this season.
Pelicans rookie Jeremiah Fears tallied 18 points and 7 assists in just 24 minutes of action.
games.context: The Pelicans have now won 11 of their last 20 games to reach 21 wins, matching their total from all of last season.

games.headline: Celtics sweep season series with Cavs as Tatum finds his rhythm
games.brief: In a heavy-hitting Eastern Conference clash, the Boston Celtics proved they still have Cleveland's number by completing a season sweep. Playing in just his second game back from a torn Achilles, Jayson Tatum showed flashes of his elite self, helping Boston build a massive 26-point lead that the Cavaliers simply couldn't overcome despite a late surge.
bullets: Jaylen Brown stuffed the stat sheet with 23 points, 9 rebounds, and 8 assists.
Jayson Tatum finished with 20 points in 27 minutes during his second game back from a torn right Achilles.
Donovan Mitchell returned from a groin injury to lead all scorers with 30 points for Cleveland.
The Cavaliers struggled mightily from deep, starting the game shooting just 2-of-22 on three-pointers in the first half.
games.context: Boston moves within three games of Detroit for the top seed in the East and secures the tiebreaker over Cleveland.

games.headline: Doncic carries Lakers past Knicks while LeBron remains sidelined
games.brief: Luka Doncic continued his scorching scoring run to help the Lakers weather the absence of LeBron James. Los Angeles jumped out to a huge lead and held firm during a cold shooting stretch in the fourth quarter, stifling a Knicks team that struggled to find its rhythm from beyond the arc all night.
bullets: Luka Doncic followed up a 44-point performance with 35 points and 8 rebounds against New York.
Austin Reaves provided a secondary spark with 25 points as LeBron James missed his second straight game.
Karl-Anthony Towns anchored the Knicks with 25 points and 16 rebounds in the loss.
The Knicks shot a dismal 8-of-34 (23.5%) from three-point range and committed 8 turnovers in the final quarter.
games.context: The Lakers have won four straight games despite injuries to LeBron James.

games.headline: Adebayo joins 10,000-point club as Heat cool off first-place Pistons
games.brief: It was a historic night in Miami as Bam Adebayo reached the 10,000-career point milestone while leading the Heat to their fifth consecutive victory. Miami took advantage of a sluggish Detroit start, building a 25-point first-half lead and never looking back, marking the first time an Eastern Conference team has won a season series against the top-seeded Pistons this year.
bullets: Bam Adebayo scored 24 points to become just the second player (joining Dwyane Wade) to score 10,000 points in a Heat uniform.
Tyler Herro led Miami with 25 points, including a personal 9-0 run in the third quarter.
Cade Cunningham paced the Pistons with 26 points and 10 assists in their fourth straight loss.
Miami held a double-digit lead for the final 38 minutes and 27 seconds of the game.
games.context: Detroit's lead at the top of the Eastern Conference has shrunk to just 2.5 games over Boston.

games.headline: Barrett reaches milestone as Raptors hand Mavs seventh straight loss
games.brief: The Dallas Mavericks' mid-season nightmare continued in Toronto as RJ Barrett celebrated a career milestone with a season-high scoring performance. The Raptors took advantage of 20 Dallas turnovers and a poor shooting night from the Mavs to snap their own losing streak, leaving Dallas with 17 losses in their last 19 games.
bullets: RJ Barrett scored 31 points and became the youngest Canadian player to reach 8,000 career points.
Daniel Gafford was a perfect 10-for-10 from the field for a season-high 21 points in the loss.
Top pick Cooper Flagg scored 17 points but earned his first career technical foul for arguing a non-call.
Toronto dominated the open floor, outscoring Dallas 21-4 in fast-break points.
games.context: The Mavericks continue to slide down the standings, having lost 17 of their last 19 games.

games.headline: Banchero dominates as Magic exploit Giannis-less Bucks
games.brief: The Orlando Magic took full advantage of Giannis Antetokounmpo’s absence, coasting to a blowout victory in Milwaukee. Paolo Banchero was unstoppable early, leading a balanced Orlando attack that never trailed and eventually built a lead as large as 31 points, keeping the Magic firmly in the hunt for a top-five seed in the East.
bullets: Paolo Banchero poured in 33 points before resting for the entire fourth quarter.
The Bucks played without Giannis Antetokounmpo, who has now missed 30 games this season due to injury and rest.
Jalen Suggs sparked a 12-0 run in the fourth quarter to put the game completely out of reach.
The Bucks have now lost five of their last six games and sit 4.5 games out of the play-in tournament.
games.context: Orlando wins its fourth straight to stay sixth in the East, while Milwaukee's playoff hopes continue to fade.

games.headline: Wembanyama and Spurs dismantle Rockets in offensive masterclass
games.brief: The San Antonio Spurs put on an offensive clinic against their Texas rivals, shooting a blistering 58% from the floor to blow past the Rockets. Victor Wembanyama set the tone early with a mix of perimeter shooting and interior dominance, helping San Antonio secure the season series and tighten their grip on the Western Conference's No. 2 seed.
bullets: Victor Wembanyama led the way with 29 points on 9-of-13 shooting in just three quarters of play.
The Spurs recorded 38 assists and made 21 three-pointers (53%) in their highest-scoring game of the season.
De'Aaron Fox orchestrated the offense with 20 points and 10 assists.
Houston allowed a season-high 145 points and a season-most 56 points in the paint.
games.context: The Spurs have won 15 of their last 16 games to move within 2.5 games of first place in the West.

games.headline: Scoot Henderson hits season high as Pacers losing streak hits nine
games.brief: Scoot Henderson delivered his best performance of the season to lead the Trail Blazers past a struggling Pacers squad. Portland blew the game open with a massive run late in the second quarter, leaving Indiana to ponder their ninth consecutive loss—a stretch defined by defensive struggles and double-digit defeats.
bullets: Scoot Henderson scored a season-high 28 points on efficient 10-of-15 shooting with zero turnovers.
The Blazers used a 25-7 run over the final five minutes of the first half to seize control.
Indiana's Jay Huff recorded 5 blocks, bringing his NBA-leading total of multi-block games to 39.
Deni Avdija returned from a six-game injury absence to contribute 18 points and 8 assists.
games.context: The Pacers' ninth straight loss is their third streak of at least eight losses this season.

games.headline: Westbrook extends triple-double record in rare Kings victory
games.brief: Russell Westbrook continues to defy Father Time, recording his 208th career triple-double to lead the Sacramento Kings to a much-needed home win. The Kings dominated the Bulls in the paint and led from start to finish, providing a bright spot in what has otherwise been a difficult stretch for the franchise.
bullets: Russell Westbrook tallied 23 points, 12 assists, and 11 rebounds for his 208th triple-double.
Westbrook is now just one assist away from passing Mark Jackson for 6th on the all-time list.
Rookie Maxine Raynaud had a standout night with 26 points and 11 rebounds.
The Kings outscored the Bulls 68-36 in the paint, while Chicago's Josh Giddey also notched a triple-double.
games.context: This was just the third win in the last 23 games for the Sacramento Kings.

games.headline: Booker's perfect night at the line snaps Hornets' road streak
games.brief: The Charlotte Hornets' impressive two-month run of road dominance finally came to an end in the desert. Devin Booker utilized a veteran's touch to get to the free-throw line 15 times without a miss, leading a shorthanded Suns team to victory and halting Charlotte's away winning streak at 10 games.
bullets: Devin Booker scored 30 points, shooting a perfect 15-of-15 from the free-throw line.
The loss snapped Charlotte's 10-game road winning streak, the longest in the NBA this season.
Hornets rookie Kon Knueppel made two 3-pointers, extending his league-leading total to 224 on the season.
Jalen Green and Collin Gillespie both chipped in 24 points for the Suns.
games.context: The Suns have won four of five as they head out for a six-game road trip.
